title: Oracle And Dell Take Aim At Midsize Businesses
author: Larry Greenemeier (InformationWeek / Network Computing)
abstract: InformationWeek/Network Computing article on the Oracle-Dell SMB pre-installation deal: Oracle granted Dell the exclusive right (through 2004 year-end) to deliver preinstalled Oracle Standard Edition One on Dell PowerEdge 2600/2650 servers running Red Hat Linux or Windows, starting at $4,108 for 5/10/25/unlimited user licenses. Aberdeen Group analyst Peter Kastner argues the pre-install model is particularly useful to SMBs, and the nine-month head start could translate into 1 million shipments for Dell — 'Dell will be able to take the exclusivity period to the bank.'
importance_rationale: Captures the 2004 Oracle-Dell SMB thrust at a key moment in the commoditization-of-enterprise-stack narrative; 1M shipment forecast was a headline-level Kastner call.
relevance_rationale: The Oracle-Dell SMB pre-install pattern expanded; Dell became global x86 leader; Oracle Standard Edition One retired in favor of SE2 in 2014.
prescience_rationale: Dell did grow dominant in x86 commodity servers; but Oracle SE One sales were eventually modest for SMB vs. SQL Server and open-source alternatives.
